title: Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)

{{Quote|I am closing this shipyard. Give me the operations codes of every newly completed warship.|Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
A '''battle''' at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]] occurred in [[3996 BBY]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. It began as a raid carried out by the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] forces of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|Crusaders]] of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]]. Qel-Droma planned to seize the many [[Starship/Legends|ships]] under construction for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and instead use them in his [[Krath Holy Crusade|bid]] for galactic domination. At Qel-Droma's behest, Krath [[Sith sorcerer|sorceress]] [[Aleema Keto]] created an [[Mind trick/Legends|illusion]] that allowed their forces to slip into the Foerost [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] unchallenged. Bolstered by the [[Army/Legends|armies]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], Qel-Droma's forces stormed the facility and secured the operational codes for the newly-completed vessels. [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Orley Vanicus]] was dispatched to interrupt the raid with a contingent of Republic reinforcements, but was too late to stop the theft of over three hundred [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. A recording of Qel-Droma from the attack was later discovered, finally leaking the secret identity of the Republic's new enemy.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|Wake up down there, Grid Seven! We've got a ship leaving hyperspace…askin' to dock for repairs to her hull! A big one, from the readings I'm getting. Maybe the hyperscan's broken.|Tower Bravo|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
By [[3996 BBY]], the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] maintained by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] at [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]] had been established as one of the oldest and most successful [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction facilities in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. For hundreds of [[Time/Legends|years]], the facility had produced [[Warship/Legends|warships]] for the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] with raw materials from the surface of Foerost, an uninhabited [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. The vessels built there were prized by the Republic as some of the most advanced warships in their fleets. More than three hundred of the ships, most either newly completed or nearly so, sat relatively unguarded in the shipyards.<ref name="TSW1" />

[[File:Foerost.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Golthar's Sky'' illusion approaches the Foerost Shipyards, seconds before revealing its true form.]]
Leader of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]], [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] desired the vessels for use in the coming [[Great Sith War/Legends|war]] between the [[Brotherhood of the Sith|Sith Brotherhood]] and the Republic. He gathered his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], bolstered by the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|Crusaders]] of his newly-sworn ally [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], and set out for the Foerost shipyards. Upon arrival, the lover of Qel-Droma, [[Sith sorcerer|sorceress]] [[Aleema Keto]], conjured a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Mind trick/Legends|illusion]] that disguised the Krath armada as a single, large vessel, called the "''[[Golthar's Sky]]''." The illusion, which thwarted the monitors' [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] systems and gave the impression of a massive vessel exiting [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], approached the shipyards and requested clearance to dock for repairs. Dock supervisors in Tower Bravo noticed the ship's density and offered to dispatch an inspection crew, as none of the Foerost docks were able to support the vessel.<ref name="TSW1" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|Well done, well done. We'll ''need'' all that armor for the engagements I have planned. Gather more ships while I ''train'' my Jedi!|Exar Kun|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
[[File:BattleofFoerost2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mandalorians in action during the battle]]
Keto dropped the illusion at that moment and revealed a fleet of [[Supremacy-class attack ship|Tetan attack ships]]. Krath-dispatched [[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s swarmed and strafed the shipyard, which resulted in the swift destruction of the facilities' defensive emplacements. [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]]-mounted [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] penetrated the modules and stormed the control decks and operations centers. Tetan [[Troop/Legends|troops]], equipped with [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]], also boarded the stations to reinforce overwhelming force of the Mandalorian offensive.<ref name="TSW1" /> While the Republic forces attempted to fend off the Krath assault in futility, one of their number, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Dace Golliard]], fled the battle in an attempt to save his own life.<ref name="KOTOR49" />

The shipyards were quickly overrun, due largely to the ferocity of the Mandalorian assault. Qel-Droma, Aleema Keto, and Mandalore the Indomitable boarded the station assumed command at the control center. The Sith Lord demanded the operational codes of the newly-completed warships, and [[Death/Legends|killed]] the first person to deny knowledge of said codes in order to demonstrate the gravity of his request. The other [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] present quickly realized their predicament and fully complied, and Qel-Droma was given the access he sought.<ref name="TSW1" />

Communication was soon established with [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and Qel-Droma's [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]]. Kun had managed to sway twenty [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to their side of the war, which Qel-Droma found to be rather meager in comparison to the two [[Army/Legends|armies]] and now the three hundred vessels that he had acquired. The Krath leader announced to Kun his plans to attack [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|seat]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power. The Krath forces then pulled out and abandoned the shipyards.<ref name="TSW1" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Captain Vanicus reporting…receiving an emergency distress signal. My fleet has been dispatched to the Foerost Shipyards, the target of yet another vicious raid. This time we had hoped to catch the mysterious pirates, to confront them with our superior military strength… but we are too late.|Captain Vanicus|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
[[File:FoerostBattleAftermath-TSW1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The aftermath of the battle]]
Upon receiving news of the attack, the Republic [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] sent a fleet under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Orley Vanicus]] to try and interrupt the raid, but the reinforcements arrived too late. All of the complete and near-complete warships had been stolen, the facilities largely destroyed—the scene was a graveyard. Corpses of slain yard workers floated among the wreckage. Vanicus resolved to salvage as much as he could and investigate the scene in the hopes of discovering the identity of the attackers.<ref name="TSW1" />

Few [[Escape pod/Legends|rescue pods]] remained to be recovered, leaving very few witnesses to the raid. Searching through the control centers, Vanicus' men discovered that the attackers had damaged as much [[Computer/Legends|computer]] hardware as possible in an attempt to erase all evidence of their identity. However, one file was found, recorded by the station's automated security systems. Vanicus, who had fought at the [[Battle of Koros Major]] alongside the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], recognized Qel-Droma—the secret was out.<ref name="TSW1" /> Qel-Droma's treachery was not the only one exposed either; the Republic also learned of Dace Golliard's absconding from the massacre at Foerost. He was branded a criminal of the Republic and spent the next several decades of his life as a fugitive.<ref name="KOTOR49" />

[[3963 BBY|Thirty-three years]] after the raid on the Foerost shipyards, Dace Golliard—who had since turned to a life of crime as a [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]]—was apprehended by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] of the Republic Navy. Karath was especially eager to capture Golliard, since the Admiral's father, [[Craddock Karath]], was one of the many Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] who had perished while Golliard had escaped to safety. Karath vowed that the outlaw would finally face the court-martial that he had tried so desperately to avoid.<ref name="KOTOR49" />

==Behind the scenes==
The events of the battle took place in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]], which was written by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and drawn by [[Artist/Legends|artist]] [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] Several of the characters and story elements of the issue were developed by previous [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] writer [[Tom Veitch]] in the preceding story arc, titled [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith'']]. The forty-ninth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' comic series, entitled [[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Demon, Part 3'']], revealed that ''Knights of the Old Republic'' character Dace Golliard participated in the battle. The death of Craddock Karath during the battle, who was the father of ''KOTOR'' mainstay Saul Karath, was also brought to light in the same issue.<ref name="KOTOR49" /> ''Demon, Part 3''{{'s}} author, [[John Jackson Miller]], identified Craddock Karath as the dock employee who was gunned down by Mandalore and Ulic Qel-Droma upon their entry into the shipyard control room.<ref name="JJM">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor49.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #49''}}</ref>

The French roleplaying magazine article "[[Le facteur X]]", which was published in [[Casus Belli 115|''Casus Belli'' 115]] without [[Lucas Licensing]] approval and thus is considered non-canon,<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> introduces the Jedi Master [[Sli'Lon Tahar]], who is killed by Ulic Qel-Droma during the battle in a [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]] to stop him from using [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to weaken the Sith attack. Tahar just had time to inform some adventurers where the tomb of an ancient Sith was located before being struck down by the fallen Jedi, but accidentally revealed it to Qel-Droma as well.<ref name="Casus Belli 115 : Le Facteur X">{{CasusBelliCite|115|Le facteur X}}</ref>
